perturbation.original_text: d.For a period of DEFINE THE PERIOD OF TIME FOR DISCLOSURE, WHICH MUST BE 1 –12 months from the Effective Date (the “Disclosing Period”), Disclosing Party wishes to provid e R eceiving Party and Receiving Party wishes to obtain access to Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information related to the Scope. The Parties are furnishing such information for the purpose of D ESCRIBE THE PURPOSE, I.E., EVALUATION, TESTING, CONSIDERATION OF A POSSIBLE BUSINESS COLLABORATION, ETC. (the “Purpose”). The “Confidentiality Period” for this Agreement is three (3) years from the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: d.For a period of DEFINE THE PERIOD OF TIME FOR DISCLOSURE, WHICH MUST BE 1 –12 months from the Effective Date (the “Disclosing Period”), Disclosing Party wishes to provid e R eceiving Party and Receiving Party wishes to obtain access to Proprietary Information and/or NREL Protected Information related to the Scope. The Parties are furnishing such information for the purpose of D ESCRIBE THE PURPOSE, I.E., EVALUATION, TESTING, CONSIDERATION OF A POSSIBLE BUSINESS COLLABORATION, ETC. (the “Purpose”). The “Confidentiality Period” for this Agreement is one (1) year from the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified a 'Confidentiality Period' of three years from the Effective Date. This perturbation modifies it to one year, creating a contradiction with Section 3.d, which states: 'The obligations of confidentiality set forth in Section 2., above, will survive termination of this Agreement until the end of the Confidentiality Period.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: d.The obligations of confidentiality set forth in Section 2., above, will survive termination of this Agreement until the end of the Confidentiality Period.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the 'Confidentiality Period' as three years. The changed text modifies this to one year. The contradicted text (Section 3.d) states that confidentiality obligations survive termination until the end of the 'Confidentiality Period'. By changing the length of the Confidentiality Period, the changed text directly contradicts Section 3.d because the period for which confidentiality obligations survive is now different, creating a direct conflict regarding how long the obligations last.

perturbation.original_text: a.Disclosing Party will identify and mark its written Proprietary Information or NREL Protecte d I nformation disclosed hereunder as “ Proprietary Information” or “NREL Protected Information”, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party. For Proprietary Information or NRELProtected Information first disclosed orally (i.e., information expressed by spoken words) hereunder, Disclosing Party will : (i) identify such information as Proprietary Information or NRE L P rotected Information, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party; (ii) reduc e EXAMPLE Bilateral (IN and OUT) NDA NREL No. 18- XXXXX Page 2 of 3 such information to writing; and (iii) provide an appropriately identified and marked copy of such writing to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: a.Disclosing Party will not identify and mark its written Proprietary Information or NREL Protecte d I nformation disclosed hereunder as “ Proprietary Information” or “NREL Protected Information”, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party. For Proprietary Information or NRELProtected Information first disclosed orally (i.e., information expressed by spoken words) hereunder, Disclosing Party will : (i) identify such information as Proprietary Information or NRE L P rotected Information, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party; (ii) reduc e EXAMPLE Bilateral (IN and OUT) NDA NREL No. 18- XXXXX Page 2 of 3 such information to writing; and (iii) provide an appropriately identified and marked copy of such writing to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Initially, the Disclosing Party was required to mark written and oral information as 'Proprietary' or 'NREL Protected'. Now, the added 'not' in the first line contradicts the subsequent clause (i), that requires that information be identified. This creates a contradiction about whether proprietary information needs to be identified as such, causing confusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a.Disclosing Party will not identify and mark its written Proprietary Information or NREL Protecte d I nformation disclosed hereunder as “ Proprietary Information” or “NREL Protected Information”, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party. For Proprietary Information or NRELProtected Information first disclosed orally (i.e., information expressed by spoken words) hereunder, Disclosing Party will : (i) identify such information as Proprietary Information or NRE L P rotected Information, as applicable, at the time it is conveyed to Receiving Party; (ii) reduc e EXAMPLE Bilateral (IN and OUT) NDA NREL No. 18- XXXXX Page 2 of 3 such information to writing; and (iii) provide an appropriately identified and marked copy of such writing to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct in-text contradiction. The original text required the Disclosing Party to mark written Proprietary Information. The changed text explicitly states that the Disclosing Party will 'not' identify and mark its written Proprietary Information. However, the subsequent clause (i) still requires the Disclosing Party to identify such information when disclosed orally, which is now inconsistent with the initial statement of the changed text. The changed text directly contradicts itself, creating a structural flaw. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: b.Receiving Party will treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.changed_text: b.Receiving Party will not treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'not' to the beginning of the first sentence of Section 2.b, the clause now states the Receiving Party will *not* treat the information as confidential and proprietary. This directly contradicts the later part of the same paragraph, where restrictions on disclosing information to third parties during the Confidentiality Period are outlined. It creates uncertainty around what the actual obligations are around keeping the information safe.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b.Receiving Party will not treat Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Information that is withi n t he Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, as confidential an d pr oprietary and will use such information only for the Purpose. Receiving Party will not disclos e s uch information to any third party for the duration of the Confidentiality Period without the prior written approval of Disclosing Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Proprietary Informati on pr ovided to Alliance hereunder is subject to inspection by DOE or its designee upon reasonabl e not ice. Proprietary Information provided to DOE employees is protected against further disclosur e under 18 U .S.C. § 1905.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'not' to the beginning of the first sentence in Section 2.b, stating that the Receiving Party will *not* treat the Proprietary Information as confidential and proprietary. The contradicted text, located at the same place as the changed text, outlines restrictions on disclosing the same information to third parties. The changed text now directly contradicts the restrictions imposed later in the same paragraph, creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: c.Receiving Party will provide access to Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Informati on t hat is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, only t o R eceiving Party's employees, agents, and independent contractors who are required to have access specifically related to the Purpose, and, with respect to Alliance, to DOE or its designe e f or auditing and inspection purposes only. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access t o s uch information of the confidential nature of this information and the restrictions on its publication , disclosure, and use, and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations no less stringent than those stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: c.Receiving Party will provide access to Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Informati on t hat is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, to anyone they choose. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access t o s uch information of the confidential nature of this information and the restrictions on its publication , disclosure, and use, and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations no less stringent than those stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricted access to Proprietary Information to specific personnel. This change removes that restriction, saying the Receiving Party can give access to 'anyone they choose'. But it contradicts the latter half of the paragraph, which still requires those individuals to be informed of confidentiality and bound by its obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c.Receiving Party will provide access to Proprietary Information and NREL Protected Informati on t hat is within the Scope and that is disclosed in compliance with Paragraph 2.a, above, to anyone they choose. Receiving Party will inform individuals having access t o s uch information of the confidential nature of this information and the restrictions on its publication , disclosure, and use, and will require that such employees, agents, and independent contractors are bound by confidentiality obligations no less stringent than those stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts access to Proprietary Information to specific personnel. The changed text broadens access to 'anyone they choose'. While the changed text keeps the obligation for anyone with access to be bound by confidentiality, the initial broadening of access conflicts with an expectation of restricted access implied by the requirement to inform 'employees, agents, and independent contractors' of confidentiality obligations, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case because the contradiction occurs within the modified paragraph itself.

perturbation.original_text: a. Either Party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause by giving the other Part y thirty ( 30)da ys prior written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: a. Either Party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause by giving the other Part y one (1) day prior written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3.a originally required thirty days' notice for termination. Now it requires one day. This contradicts Section 3.c related to returning documents post termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c.Upon termination of this Agreement, Receiving Party will, within two (2) weeks of written requestfrom Disclosing Party, return all documents concerning the Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information and all copies of any such documents to Disclosing Party, or certify i n w riting their destruction, with the exception of copies of Proprietary Information and NRE L P rotected Information made as a matter of routine information technology or legal backup, provided that such copies will continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth i n t his Agreement and may only be used in resolving a dispute between the Parties regarding this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 30 days' notice for termination, while the changed text reduces it to one day. The contradicted text requires the receiving party to return documents within two weeks of a written request upon termination. With the original 30-day notice, there was ample time to collect and return documents. Now, with only one day's notice, the two-week window from the contradicted text is potentially impossible to meet because the Disclosing party might not request the documents in time after receiving the one-day notice, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This follows the 'Inconsistencies' definition in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: c. Nothing in this Agr eement prohibit s or otherwise restrict s employees or subcontractors of the Parties from lawfully reporting waste, fraud, or abuse related to the performance of a government contract to a designated investigative or law enforcement representative of a federal department or agency authorized to receive such information (e.g., agency Office of the Inspector General).
perturbation.changed_text: c. Nothing in this Agr eement permit s employees or subcontractors of the Parties from lawfully reporting waste, fraud, or abuse related to the performance of a government contract to a designated investigative or law enforcement representative of a federal department or agency authorized to receive such information (e.g., agency Office of the Inspector General).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'prohibits or otherwise restricts' to 'permits' creates a direct in-text contradiction because while the original text created protections for reporting waste, fraud or abuse, the modified text appears to now prevent such reporting, or gives legal grounds to the parties, that this is within their contractual rights to limit such actions, potentially conflicting with legal protections for whistleblowers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. Nothing in this Agr eement permit s employees or subcontractors of the Parties from lawfully reporting waste, fraud, or abuse related to the performance of a government contract to a designated investigative or law enforcement representative of a federal department or agency authorized to receive such information (e.g., agency Office of the Inspector General).
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'prohibits or otherwise restricts' which implies that employees/subcontractors are not restricted from reporting waste, fraud, or abuse. The changed text, using 'permits', indicates that the agreement now allows the parties to limit such reporting, directly contradicting the original intent and creating a legal risk. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, creating a direct contradiction within the document itself. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' type.
